metadata.name: mailchimp__automation_activities
metadata.comment: Each record represents an activity taken in relation to a automation email.

action_type.name: action_type
action_type.comment: One of the following actions: 'open', 'click', or 'bounce'

automation_email_id.name: automation_email_id
automation_email_id.comment: The ID for the automation email that relates to the activity.

member_id.name: member_id
member_id.comment: The ID of the member the activity relates to.

list_id.name: list_id
list_id.comment: The ID of the list the email was sent to.

activity_timestamp.name: activity_timestamp
activity_timestamp.comment: The timestamp when the activity occurred.

ip_address.name: ip_address
ip_address.comment: The IP address recorded for the action.

url.name: url
url.comment: If the action is a 'click', the URL on which the member clicked.

bounce_type.name: bounce_type
bounce_type.comment: If the action is a 'bounce', the type of bounce received: 'hard', 'soft'.

automation_id.name: automation_id
automation_id.comment: The ID for the automation that relates to the activity.

segment_id.name: segment_id
segment_id.comment: The ID for the Segment that relates to the activity.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: mailchimp__automation_emails
metadata.comment: Each record represents an automation email (that make up automations), enriched with click, open and unsubscribe metrics. (Enabled if you are using automations.)

automation_email_id.name: automation_email_id
automation_email_id.comment: The ID for the automation email that relates to the activity.

automation_id.name: automation_id
automation_id.comment: The ID of the automation an email relates to.

created_timestamp.name: created_timestamp
created_timestamp.comment: The date and time the automation email was created.

started_timestamp.name: started_timestamp
started_timestamp.comment: The date and time the automation email was started.

send_timestamp.name: send_timestamp
send_timestamp.comment: The date and time the automation email was sent.

from_name.name: from_name
from_name.comment: The 'from' name on the automation email (not an email address).

reply_to.name: reply_to
reply_to.comment: The reply-to email address for the automation.

status.name: status
status.comment: The current status of the automation email.

subject_line.name: subject_line
subject_line.comment: The subject line for the automation email.

title.name: title
title.comment: The title of the automation email.

to_name.name: to_name
to_name.comment: The automation email's custom 'To' name. Typically the first name merge field.

archive_url.name: archive_url
archive_url.comment: The link to the automation email's archive version in ISO 8601 format.

authenticate.name: authenticate
authenticate.comment: Whether Mailchimp authenticated the automation email. Defaults to true.

auto_footer.name: auto_footer
auto_footer.comment: Automatically append Mailchimp's default footer to the automation email.

auto_tweet.name: auto_tweet
auto_tweet.comment: Automatically tweet a link to the automation email archive page when the automation email is sent.

clicktale.name: clicktale
clicktale.comment: The custom slug for ClickTale tracking (max of 50 bytes).

content_type.name: content_type
content_type.comment: How the automation email's content is put together ('template', 'drag_and_drop', 'html', 'url').

delay_action.name: delay_action
delay_action.comment: The action that triggers the delay of an Automation email.

delay_action_description.name: delay_action_description
delay_action_description.comment: The user-friendly description of the action that triggers an Automation email.

delay_amount.name: delay_amount
delay_amount.comment: The delay amount for an Automation email.

delay_direction.name: delay_direction
delay_direction.comment: Whether the delay settings describe before or after the delay action of an Automation email.

delay_full_description.name: delay_full_description
delay_full_description.comment: The user-friendly description of the delay and trigger action settings for an Automation email.

delay_type.name: delay_type
delay_type.comment: The type of delay for an Automation email.

drag_and_drop.name: drag_and_drop
drag_and_drop.comment: Whether the automation email uses the drag-and-drop editor.

fb_comments.name: fb_comments
fb_comments.comment: Allows Facebook comments on the automation email (also force-enables the Campaign Archive toolbar). Defaults to true.

folder_id.name: folder_id
folder_id.comment: If the automation email is listed in a folder, the ID for that folder.

google_analytics.name: google_analytics
google_analytics.comment: The custom slug for Google Analytics tracking (max of 50 bytes).

inline_css.name: inline_css
inline_css.comment: Automatically inline the CSS included with the automation email content.

position.name: position
position.comment: The position of an Automation email in a workflow.

template_id.name: template_id
template_id.comment: The ID for the template used in this automation email.

timewarp.name: timewarp
timewarp.comment: Send this automation email using Timewarpe_this

track_html_clicks.name: track_html_clicks
track_html_clicks.comment: Whether to track clicks in the HTML version of the automation email. Defaults to true. Cannot be set to false for variate campaigns.

track_opens.name: track_opens
track_opens.comment: Whether to track opens. Defaults to true. Cannot be set to false for variate automation emails.

track_text_clicks.name: track_text_clicks
track_text_clicks.comment: Whether to track clicks in the plain-text version of the automation email. Defaults to true. Cannot be set to false for variate campaigns.

use_conversation.name: use_conversation
use_conversation.comment: Use Mailchimp Conversation feature to manage out-of-office replies.

list_id.name: list_id
list_id.comment: The ID of the List this automation email is being sent to.

segment_id.name: segment_id
segment_id.comment: The ID of the Segment this automation email is being sent to.

sends.name: sends
sends.comment: The number of sent emails from automations.

opens.name: opens
opens.comment:
The number of open events from automation emails. 
The same email being opened multiple times is included in this metric.

clicks.name: clicks
clicks.comment:
The number of click events from automation emails. 
The same email being clicked multiple times is included in this metric.

unique_opens.name: unique_opens
unique_opens.comment:
The unique number of emails opened from automation emails. 
If a given email to a user was opened multiple times, it will only count once in this metric.
This is the metric for opens that is reported by Mailchimp.

unique_clicks.name: unique_clicks
unique_clicks.comment:
The unique number of emails clicked from automation emails. 
If a given email to a user was clicked multiple times, it will only count once in this metric.
This is the metric for clicks that is reported by Mailchimp.

unsubscribes.name: unsubscribes
unsubscribes.comment: The number of users who have unsubscribed from automation emails.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: mailchimp__automations
metadata.comment: Each record represents an automation in Mailchimp, enriched with click, open and unsubscribe metrics. (Enabled if you are using automations.)

automation_id.name: automation_id
automation_id.comment: The ID of the automation in Mailchimp.

list_id.name: list_id
list_id.comment: The ID of the List an automation was sent to.

segment_id.name: segment_id
segment_id.comment: The ID of the Segment an automation was sent to.

segment_text.name: segment_text
segment_text.comment: Description of the segment. Formatted as a string marked up with HTML.

started_timestamp.name: started_timestamp
started_timestamp.comment: The date and time the Automation was started.

created_timestamp.name: created_timestamp
created_timestamp.comment: The date and time the Automation was created.

status.name: status
status.comment: The current status of the Automation. Possible Values: save, paused, sending

sends.name: sends
sends.comment: The number of sent emails from automations.

opens.name: opens
opens.comment:
The number of open events from automation emails. 
The same email being opened multiple times is included in this metric.

clicks.name: clicks
clicks.comment:
The number of click events from automation emails. 
The same email being clicked multiple times is included in this metric.

unique_opens.name: unique_opens
unique_opens.comment:
The unique number of emails opened from automation emails. 
If a given email to a user was opened multiple times, it will only count once in this metric.
This is the metric for opens that is reported by Mailchimp.

unique_clicks.name: unique_clicks
unique_clicks.comment:
The unique number of emails clicked from automation emails. 
If a given email to a user was clicked multiple times, it will only count once in this metric.
This is the metric for clicks that is reported by Mailchimp.

unsubscribes.name: unsubscribes
unsubscribes.comment: The number of users who have unsubscribed from automation emails.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: mailchimp__campaign_activities
metadata.comment: Each record represents an activity taken in relation to a campaign email, enriched with data about when the campaign was sent and the lag between send and the activity.

action_type.name: action_type
action_type.comment: One of the following actions: 'open', 'click', or 'bounce'

campaign_id.name: campaign_id
campaign_id.comment: The unique ID for the sent campaign.

member_id.name: member_id
member_id.comment: The ID of the member the activity relates to.

list_id.name: list_id
list_id.comment: The ID of the list the email was sent to.

activity_timestamp.name: activity_timestamp
activity_timestamp.comment: The timestamp when the activity occurred.

ip_address.name: ip_address
ip_address.comment: The IP address recorded for the action.

url.name: url
url.comment: If the action is a 'click', the URL on which the member clicked.

bounce_type.name: bounce_type
bounce_type.comment: If the action is a 'bounce', the type of bounce received: 'hard', 'soft'.

combination_id.name: combination_id
combination_id.comment: The ID of the combination the member was part of for a campaign.

email_id.name: email_id
email_id.comment: The ID of the email sent. Surrogate key generated in dbt from 'campaign_id' and 'member_id'.

send_timestamp.name: send_timestamp
send_timestamp.comment: The time the campaign was sent.

time_since_send_minutes.name: time_since_send_minutes
time_since_send_minutes.comment: The time from send to activity in minutes.

time_since_send_hours.name: time_since_send_hours
time_since_send_hours.comment: The time from send to activity in hours.

time_since_send_days.name: time_since_send_days
time_since_send_days.comment: The time from send to activity in days.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: mailchimp__campaign_recipients
metadata.comment: Each record represents the send of a campaign email, enriched with click, open and unsubscribe metrics.

campaign_id.name: campaign_id
campaign_id.comment: The ID of the campaign a sent email is from.

member_id.name: member_id
member_id.comment: The ID of the member an email was sent to.

combination_id.name: combination_id
combination_id.comment: The ID of the combination the member was part of for a campaign.

list_id.name: list_id
list_id.comment: The ID of the list the campaign was sent to.

email_id.name: email_id
email_id.comment: Surrogate key hashed on source_relation, campaign_id and member_id.

segment_id.name: segment_id
segment_id.comment: The ID of the segment the campaign was sent to.

send_timestamp.name: send_timestamp
send_timestamp.comment: The date and time a campaign was sent.

opens.name: opens
opens.comment:
The number of open events from campaign emails. 
The same email being opened multiple times is included in this metric.

unique_opens.name: unique_opens
unique_opens.comment:
The unique number of emails opened from campaign emails. 
If a given email to a user was opened multiple times, it will only count once in this metric.
This is the metric for opens that is reported by Mailchimp.

clicks.name: clicks
clicks.comment:
The number of click events from campaign emails. 
The same email being clicked multiple times is included in this metric.

unique_clicks.name: unique_clicks
unique_clicks.comment:
The unique number of emails clicked from campaign emails. 
If a given email to a user was clicked multiple times, it will only count once in this metric.
This is the metric for clicks that is reported by Mailchimp.

was_opened.name: was_opened
was_opened.comment: Whether the email sent was opened.

was_clicked.name: was_clicked
was_clicked.comment: Whether the email sent was clicked.

first_open_timestamp.name: first_open_timestamp
first_open_timestamp.comment: The timestamp of the first open for an email.

time_to_open_minutes.name: time_to_open_minutes
time_to_open_minutes.comment: The time from send to open in minutes.

time_to_open_hours.name: time_to_open_hours
time_to_open_hours.comment: The time from send to open in hours.

time_to_open_days.name: time_to_open_days
time_to_open_days.comment: The time from send to open in days.

was_unsubscribed.name: was_unsubscribed
was_unsubscribed.comment: Whether the user unsubscribed via the sent email.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: mailchimp__campaigns
metadata.comment: Each record represents a campaign in Mailchimp, enriched with click, open and unsubscribe metrics.

campaign_id.name: campaign_id
campaign_id.comment: The ID of the campaign in Mailchimp.

segment_id.name: segment_id
segment_id.comment: The ID of the Segment a campaign was sent to.

create_timestamp.name: create_timestamp
create_timestamp.comment: The date and time the campaign was created.

send_timestamp.name: send_timestamp
send_timestamp.comment: The date and time a campaign was sent.

list_id.name: list_id
list_id.comment: The ID of the List a campaign was sent to.

reply_to_email.name: reply_to_email
reply_to_email.comment: The reply-to email address for the campaign.

campaign_type.name: campaign_type
campaign_type.comment: The type of campaign sent.  Possible values: regular, plaintext, absplit, variate, rss.

archive_url.name: archive_url
archive_url.comment: The link to the campaign's archive version in ISO 8601 format.

authenticate.name: authenticate
authenticate.comment: Whether Mailchimp authenticated the campaign. Defaults to true.

auto_footer.name: auto_footer
auto_footer.comment: Automatically append Mailchimp's default footer to the campaign.

auto_tweet.name: auto_tweet
auto_tweet.comment: Automatically tweet a link to the campaign archive page when the campaign is sent.

clicktale.name: clicktale
clicktale.comment: The custom slug for ClickTale tracking (max of 50 bytes).

content_type.name: content_type
content_type.comment: How the campaign's content is put together ('template', 'drag_and_drop', 'html', 'url').

drag_and_drop.name: drag_and_drop
drag_and_drop.comment: Whether the campaign uses the drag-and-drop editor.

fb_comments.name: fb_comments
fb_comments.comment: Allows Facebook comments on the campaign (also force-enables the Campaign Archive toolbar). Defaults to true.

folder_id.name: folder_id
folder_id.comment: If the campaign is listed in a folder, the ID for that folder.

from_name.name: from_name
from_name.comment: The 'from' name on the campaign (not an email address).

google_analytics.name: google_analytics
google_analytics.comment: The custom slug for Google Analytics tracking (max of 50 bytes).

inline_css.name: inline_css
inline_css.comment: Automatically inline the CSS included with the campaign content.

long_archive_url.name: long_archive_url
long_archive_url.comment: The original link to the campaign's archive version.

status.name: status
status.comment: The current status of the campaign.

subject_line.name: subject_line
subject_line.comment: The subject line for the campaign.

template_id.name: template_id
template_id.comment: The ID for the template used in this campaign.

test_size.name: test_size
test_size.comment: The percentage of recipients to send the test combinations to, must be a value between 10 and 100.

to_name.name: to_name
to_name.comment: The campaign's custom 'To' name. Typically the first name merge field.

track_html_clicks.name: track_html_clicks
track_html_clicks.comment: Whether to track clicks in the HTML version of the campaign. Defaults to true. Cannot be set to false for variate campaigns.

track_opens.name: track_opens
track_opens.comment: Whether to track opens. Defaults to true. Cannot be set to false for variate campaigns.

track_text_clicks.name: track_text_clicks
track_text_clicks.comment: Whether to track clicks in the plain-text version of the campaign. Defaults to true. Cannot be set to false for variate campaigns.

use_conversation.name: use_conversation
use_conversation.comment: Use Mailchimp Conversation feature to manage out-of-office replies.

wait_time.name: wait_time
wait_time.comment: The number of minutes to wait before choosing the winning campaign. The value of wait_time must be greater than 0 and in whole hours, specified in minutes.

winner_criteria.name: winner_criteria
winner_criteria.comment: The combination that performs the best.  This may be determined automatically by click rate, open rate, or total revenue—or  you may choose manually based on the reporting data you find the most valuable.  For Multivariate Campaigns testing send_time, winner_criteria is ignored.  For Multivariate Campaigns with 'manual' as the winner_criteria, the winner must be chosen in the Mailchimp web application.

winning_campaign_id.name: winning_campaign_id
winning_campaign_id.comment: ID of the campaign that was sent to the remaining recipients based on the winning combination.

sends.name: sends
sends.comment: The number of sent emails from campaigns.

opens.name: opens
opens.comment:
The number of open events from campaign emails. 
The same email being opened multiple times is included in this metric.

clicks.name: clicks
clicks.comment:
The number of click events from campaign emails. 
The same email being clicked multiple times is included in this metric.

unique_opens.name: unique_opens
unique_opens.comment:
The unique number of emails opened from campaign emails. 
If a given email to a user was opened multiple times, it will only count once in this metric.
This is the metric for opens that is reported by Mailchimp.

unique_clicks.name: unique_clicks
unique_clicks.comment:
The unique number of emails clicked from campaign emails. 
If a given email to a user was clicked multiple times, it will only count once in this metric.
This is the metric for clicks that is reported by Mailchimp.

unsubscribes.name: unsubscribes
unsubscribes.comment: The number of users who have unsubscribed from campaign emails.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: mailchimp__lists
metadata.comment: Each record represents a list in Mailchimp, enriched with campaign metrics, (optionally) automation metrics and information about members.

list_id.name: list_id
list_id.comment: The unique ID of a list.

date_created.name: date_created
date_created.comment: The date and time that a list was created.

list_rating.name: list_rating
list_rating.comment: An auto-generated activity score for the list (0-5).

contact_address_1.name: contact_address_1
contact_address_1.comment: The street address for the list contact.

contact_address_2.name: contact_address_2
contact_address_2.comment: The street address for the list contact.

contact_city.name: contact_city
contact_city.comment: The city for the list contact.

contact_company.name: contact_company
contact_company.comment: The company name for the list.

contact_country.name: contact_country
contact_country.comment: A two-character ISO3166 country code. Defaults to US if invalid.

contact_state.name: contact_state
contact_state.comment: The state for the list contact.

contact_zip.name: contact_zip
contact_zip.comment: The postal or zip code for the list contact.

default_from_email.name: default_from_email
default_from_email.comment: The default from email for campaigns sent to a list.

default_from_name.name: default_from_name
default_from_name.comment: The default from name for campaigns sent to a list.

default_language.name: default_language
default_language.comment: The default language for a lists's forms.

default_subject.name: default_subject
default_subject.comment: The default subject line for campaigns sent to a list.

email_type_option.name: email_type_option
email_type_option.comment: Whether the list supports multiple formats for emails.  When set to true, subscribers can choose whether they want to receive HTML or plain-text emails.  When set to false, subscribers will receive HTML emails, with a plain-text alternative backup.

notify_on_subscribe.name: notify_on_subscribe
notify_on_subscribe.comment: The email address to send subscribe notifications to.

notify_on_unsubscribe.name: notify_on_unsubscribe
notify_on_unsubscribe.comment: The email address to send unsubscribe notifications to.

permission_reminder.name: permission_reminder
permission_reminder.comment: The permission reminder for a list.

subscribe_url_long.name: subscribe_url_long
subscribe_url_long.comment: The full version of a list's subscribe form (host will vary).

subscribe_url_short.name: subscribe_url_short
subscribe_url_short.comment: Mailchimp's EepURL shortened version of a list's subscribe form.

use_archive_bar.name: use_archive_bar
use_archive_bar.comment: Whether campaigns for this list use the Archive Bar in archives by default.

visibility.name: visibility
visibility.comment: Whether this list is public or private.

count_members.name: count_members
count_members.comment: The number of members in a list.

most_recent_signup_timestamp.name: most_recent_signup_timestamp
most_recent_signup_timestamp.comment: The date of the most recent signup to a list.

campaign_sends.name: campaign_sends
campaign_sends.comment: The number of sent emails from campaigns.

campaign_opens.name: campaign_opens
campaign_opens.comment:
The number of open events from campaign emails. 
The same email being opened multiple times is included in this metric.

campaign_clicks.name: campaign_clicks
campaign_clicks.comment:
The number of click events from campaign emails. 
The same email being clicked multiple times is included in this metric.

campaign_unique_opens.name: campaign_unique_opens
campaign_unique_opens.comment:
The unique number of emails opened from campaign emails. 
If a given email to a user was opened multiple times, it will only count once in this metric.
This is the metric for opens that is reported by Mailchimp.

campaign_unique_clicks.name: campaign_unique_clicks
campaign_unique_clicks.comment:
The unique number of emails clicked from campaign emails. 
If a given email to a user was clicked multiple times, it will only count once in this metric.
This is the metric for clicks that is reported by Mailchimp.

campaign_unsubscribes.name: campaign_unsubscribes
campaign_unsubscribes.comment: The number of users who have unsubscribed from campaign emails.

automation_sends.name: automation_sends
automation_sends.comment: The number of sent emails from automations.

automation_opens.name: automation_opens
automation_opens.comment:
The number of open events from automation emails. 
The same email being opened multiple times is included in this metric.

automation_clicks.name: automation_clicks
automation_clicks.comment:
The number of click events from automation emails. 
The same email being clicked multiple times is included in this metric.

automation_unique_opens.name: automation_unique_opens
automation_unique_opens.comment:
The unique number of emails opened from automation emails. 
If a given email to a user was opened multiple times, it will only count once in this metric.
This is the metric for opens that is reported by Mailchimp.

automation_unique_clicks.name: automation_unique_clicks
automation_unique_clicks.comment:
The unique number of emails clicked from automation emails. 
If a given email to a user was clicked multiple times, it will only count once in this metric.
This is the metric for clicks that is reported by Mailchimp.

automation_unsubscribes.name: automation_unsubscribes
automation_unsubscribes.comment: The number of users who have unsubscribed from automation emails.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: mailchimp__members
metadata.comment: Each record represents a member in Mailchimp, enriched with campaign metrics and (optionally) automation metrics.

email_type.name: email_type
email_type.comment: Type of email this member asked to get ('html' or 'text').

status.name: status
status.comment: Subscriber's current status.  Possible Values: subscribed, unsubscribed, cleaned, pending, transactional, archived

list_id.name: list_id
list_id.comment: The ID of the list the member belongs to.

signup_timestamp.name: signup_timestamp
signup_timestamp.comment: The date and time the subscriber signed up for the list.

opt_in_timestamp.name: opt_in_timestamp
opt_in_timestamp.comment: The date and time the subscribe confirmed their opt-in status.

last_changed_timestamp.name: last_changed_timestamp
last_changed_timestamp.comment: The date and time the member's info was last changed.

country_code.name: country_code
country_code.comment: The member's unique code for the location country.

dstoff.name: dstoff
dstoff.comment: The member's offset for timezones where daylight saving time is observed.

gmtoff.name: gmtoff
gmtoff.comment: The member's time difference in hours from GMT.

opt_in_ip_address.name: opt_in_ip_address
opt_in_ip_address.comment: The IP address the subscriber used to confirm their opt-in status.

signup_ip_address.name: signup_ip_address
signup_ip_address.comment: IP address the subscriber signed up from.

member_rating.name: member_rating
member_rating.comment: Star rating for this member, between 1 and 5.

timezone.name: timezone
timezone.comment: The timezone for the member's location.

unique_email_id.name: unique_email_id
unique_email_id.comment: An identifier for the address across all of Mailchimp.

campaign_sends.name: campaign_sends
campaign_sends.comment: The number of sent emails from campaigns.

campaign_opens.name: campaign_opens
campaign_opens.comment:
The number of open events from campaign emails. 
The same email being opened multiple times is included in this metric.

campaign_clicks.name: campaign_clicks
campaign_clicks.comment:
The number of click events from campaign emails. 
The same email being clicked multiple times is included in this metric.

campaign_unique_opens.name: campaign_unique_opens
campaign_unique_opens.comment:
The unique number of emails opened from campaign emails. 
If a given email to a user was opened multiple times, it will only count once in this metric.
This is the metric for opens that is reported by Mailchimp.

campaign_unique_clicks.name: campaign_unique_clicks
campaign_unique_clicks.comment:
The unique number of emails clicked from campaign emails. 
If a given email to a user was clicked multiple times, it will only count once in this metric.
This is the metric for clicks that is reported by Mailchimp.

campaign_unsubscribes.name: campaign_unsubscribes
campaign_unsubscribes.comment: The number of users who have unsubscribed from campaign emails.

automation_sends.name: automation_sends
automation_sends.comment: The number of sent emails from automations.

automation_opens.name: automation_opens
automation_opens.comment:
The number of open events from automation emails. 
The same email being opened multiple times is included in this metric.

automation_clicks.name: automation_clicks
automation_clicks.comment:
The number of click events from automation emails. 
The same email being clicked multiple times is included in this metric.

automation_unique_opens.name: automation_unique_opens
automation_unique_opens.comment:
The unique number of emails opened from automation emails. 
If a given email to a user was opened multiple times, it will only count once in this metric.
This is the metric for opens that is reported by Mailchimp.

automation_unique_clicks.name: automation_unique_clicks
automation_unique_clicks.comment:
The unique number of emails clicked from automation emails. 
If a given email to a user was clicked multiple times, it will only count once in this metric.
This is the metric for clicks that is reported by Mailchimp.

automation_unsubscribes.name: automation_unsubscribes
automation_unsubscribes.comment: The number of users who have unsubscribed from automation emails.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: mailchimp__segments

This is the metric for opens that is reported by Mailchimp.

metadata.name: stg_mailchimp__automation_activities
metadata.comment: Each record represents an activity taken in relation to a automation email.

action_type.name: action_type
action_type.comment: One of the following actions: 'open', 'click', or 'bounce'

automation_email_id.name: automation_email_id
automation_email_id.comment: The ID for the automation email that relates to the activity.

member_id.name: member_id
member_id.comment: The ID of the member the activity relates to.

list_id.name: list_id
list_id.comment: The ID of the list the email was sent to.

activity_timestamp.name: activity_timestamp
activity_timestamp.comment: The timestamp when the activity occurred.

ip_address.name: ip_address
ip_address.comment: The IP address recorded for the action.

url.name: url
url.comment: If the action is a 'click', the URL on which the member clicked.

bounce_type.name: bounce_type
bounce_type.comment: If the action is a 'bounce', the type of bounce received: 'hard', 'soft'.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_mailchimp__automation_emails
metadata.comment: Each record represents an automation email (that make up automations).

automation_email_id.name: automation_email_id
automation_email_id.comment: The ID of the automation email.

automation_id.name: automation_id
automation_id.comment: The ID of the automation an email relates to.

created_timestamp.name: created_timestamp
created_timestamp.comment: The date and time the automation email was created.

started_timestamp.name: started_timestamp
started_timestamp.comment: The date and time the automation email was started.

send_timestamp.name: send_timestamp
send_timestamp.comment: The date and time the automation email was sent.

from_name.name: from_name
from_name.comment: The 'from' name on the automation email (not an email address).

reply_to.name: reply_to
reply_to.comment: The reply-to email address for the automation.

status.name: status
status.comment: The current status of the automation email.

subject_line.name: subject_line
subject_line.comment: The subject line for the automation email.

title.name: title
title.comment: The title of the automation email.

to_name.name: to_name
to_name.comment: The automation email's custom 'To' name. Typically the first name merge field.

archive_url.name: archive_url
archive_url.comment: The link to the automation email's archive version in ISO 8601 format.

authenticate.name: authenticate
authenticate.comment: Whether Mailchimp authenticated the automation email. Defaults to true.

auto_footer.name: auto_footer
auto_footer.comment: Automatically append Mailchimp's default footer to the automation email.

auto_tweet.name: auto_tweet
auto_tweet.comment: Automatically tweet a link to the automation email archive page when the automation email is sent.

clicktale.name: clicktale
clicktale.comment: The custom slug for ClickTale tracking (max of 50 bytes).

content_type.name: content_type
content_type.comment: How the automation email's content is put together ('template', 'drag_and_drop', 'html', 'url').

delay_action.name: delay_action
delay_action.comment: The action that triggers the delay of an Automation email.

delay_action_description.name: delay_action_description
delay_action_description.comment: The user-friendly description of the action that triggers an Automation email.

delay_amount.name: delay_amount
delay_amount.comment: The delay amount for an Automation email.

delay_direction.name: delay_direction
delay_direction.comment: Whether the delay settings describe before or after the delay action of an Automation email.

delay_full_description.name: delay_full_description
delay_full_description.comment: The user-friendly description of the delay and trigger action settings for an Automation email.

delay_type.name: delay_type
delay_type.comment: The type of delay for an Automation email.

drag_and_drop.name: drag_and_drop
drag_and_drop.comment: Whether the automation email uses the drag-and-drop editor.

fb_comments.name: fb_comments
fb_comments.comment: Allows Facebook comments on the automation email (also force-enables the Campaign Archive toolbar). Defaults to true.

folder_id.name: folder_id
folder_id.comment: If the automation email is listed in a folder, the ID for that folder.

google_analytics.name: google_analytics
google_analytics.comment: The custom slug for Google Analytics tracking (max of 50 bytes).

inline_css.name: inline_css
inline_css.comment: Automatically inline the CSS included with the automation email content.

position.name: position
position.comment: The position of an Automation email in a workflow.

template_id.name: template_id
template_id.comment: The ID for the template used in this automation email.

timewarp.name: timewarp
timewarp.comment: Send this automation email using Timewarpe_this

track_html_clicks.name: track_html_clicks
track_html_clicks.comment: Whether to track clicks in the HTML version of the automation email. Defaults to true. Cannot be set to false for variate campaigns.

track_opens.name: track_opens
track_opens.comment: Whether to track opens. Defaults to true. Cannot be set to false for variate automation emails.

track_text_clicks.name: track_text_clicks
track_text_clicks.comment: Whether to track clicks in the plain-text version of the automation email. Defaults to true. Cannot be set to false for variate campaigns.

use_conversation.name: use_conversation
use_conversation.comment: Use Mailchimp Conversation feature to manage out-of-office replies.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

list_id.name: list_id

automation_recipient_id.name: automation_recipient_id
automation_recipient_id.comment: Surrogate key hashed on source_relation, member_id and automation_email_id

list_id.name: list_id
list_id.comment: The ID of the List an automation was sent to.

segment_id.name: segment_id
segment_id.comment: The ID of the Segment an automation was sent to.

segment_text.name: segment_text
segment_text.comment: The field describing the Segment an automation was sent to.

started_timestamp.name: started_timestamp
started_timestamp.comment: The date and time the Automation was started.

created_timestamp.name: created_timestamp
created_timestamp.comment: The date and time the Automation was created.

status.name: status
status.comment: The current status of the Automation. Possible Values: save, paused, sending

campaign_id.name: campaign_id

member_id.name: member_id

list_id.name: list_id

activity_timestamp.name: activity_timestamp
activity_timestamp.comment: The timestamp when the activity occurred.

ip_address.name: ip_address
ip_address.comment: The IP address recorded for the action.

url.name: url
url.comment: If the action is a 'click', the URL on which the member clicked.

bounce_type.name: bounce_type
bounce_type.comment: If the action is a 'bounce', the type of bounce received: 'hard', 'soft'.

combination_id.name: combination_id
combination_id.comment: The ID of the combination the member was part of for a campaign.

email_id.name: email_id
email_id.comment: The ID of the email sent. (Created in dbt, not Mailchimp.)

member_id.name: member_id
member_id.comment: The ID of the member an email was sent to.

combination_id.name: combination_id
combination_id.comment: The ID of the combination the member was part of for a campaign.

list_id.name: list_id
list_id.comment: The ID of the list the campaign was sent to.

metadata.name: stg_mailchimp__campaigns
metadata.comment: Each record represents a campaign in Mailchimp.

campaign_id.name: campaign_id
campaign_id.comment: The ID of the campaign in Mailchimp.

segment_id.name: segment_id
segment_id.comment: The ID of the Segment a campaign was sent to.

create_timestamp.name: create_timestamp
create_timestamp.comment: The date and time the campaign was created.

send_timestamp.name: send_timestamp
send_timestamp.comment: The date and time a campaign was sent.

list_id.name: list_id
list_id.comment: The ID of the List a campaign was sent to.

reply_to_email.name: reply_to_email
reply_to_email.comment: The reply-to email address for the campaign.

campaign_type.name: campaign_type
campaign_type.comment: The type of campaign sent.  Possible values: regular, plaintext, absplit, variate, rss.

archive_url.name: archive_url
archive_url.comment: The link to the campaign's archive version in ISO 8601 format.

authenticate.name: authenticate
authenticate.comment: Whether Mailchimp authenticated the campaign. Defaults to true.

auto_footer.name: auto_footer
auto_footer.comment: Automatically append Mailchimp's default footer to the campaign.

auto_tweet.name: auto_tweet
auto_tweet.comment: Automatically tweet a link to the campaign archive page when the campaign is sent.

clicktale.name: clicktale
clicktale.comment: The custom slug for ClickTale tracking (max of 50 bytes).

content_type.name: content_type
content_type.comment: How the campaign's content is put together ('template', 'drag_and_drop', 'html', 'url').

drag_and_drop.name: drag_and_drop
drag_and_drop.comment: Whether the campaign uses the drag-and-drop editor.

fb_comments.name: fb_comments
fb_comments.comment: Allows Facebook comments on the campaign (also force-enables the Campaign Archive toolbar). Defaults to true.

folder_id.name: folder_id
folder_id.comment: If the campaign is listed in a folder, the ID for that folder.

from_name.name: from_name
from_name.comment: The 'from' name on the campaign (not an email address).

google_analytics.name: google_analytics
google_analytics.comment: The custom slug for Google Analytics tracking (max of 50 bytes).

inline_css.name: inline_css
inline_css.comment: Automatically inline the CSS included with the campaign content.

long_archive_url.name: long_archive_url
long_archive_url.comment: The original link to the campaign's archive version.

status.name: status
status.comment: The current status of the campaign.

subject_line.name: subject_line
subject_line.comment: The subject line for the campaign.

template_id.name: template_id
template_id.comment: The ID for the template used in this campaign.

test_size.name: test_size
test_size.comment: The percentage of recipients to send the test combinations to, must be a value between 10 and 100.

to_name.name: to_name
to_name.comment: The campaign's custom 'To' name. Typically the first name merge field.

track_html_clicks.name: track_html_clicks
track_html_clicks.comment: Whether to track clicks in the HTML version of the campaign. Defaults to true. Cannot be set to false for variate campaigns.

track_opens.name: track_opens
track_opens.comment: Whether to track opens. Defaults to true. Cannot be set to false for variate campaigns.

track_text_clicks.name: track_text_clicks
track_text_clicks.comment: Whether to track clicks in the plain-text version of the campaign. Defaults to true. Cannot be set to false for variate campaigns.

use_conversation.name: use_conversation
use_conversation.comment: Use Mailchimp Conversation feature to manage out-of-office replies.

wait_time.name: wait_time
wait_time.comment: The number of minutes to wait before choosing the winning campaign. The value of wait_time must be greater than 0 and in whole hours, specified in minutes.

winner_criteria.name: winner_criteria
winner_criteria.comment: The combination that performs the best.  This may be determined automatically by click rate, open rate, or total revenue—or  you may choose manually based on the reporting data you find the most valuable.  For Multivariate Campaigns testing send_time, winner_criteria is ignored.  For Multivariate Campaigns with 'manual' as the winner_criteria, the winner must be chosen in the Mailchimp web application.

winning_campaign_id.name: winning_campaign_id
winning_campaign_id.comment: ID of the campaign that was sent to the remaining recipients based on the winning combination.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_mailchimp__lists
metadata.comment: Each record represents a list in Mailchimp.

list_id.name: list_id
list_id.comment: The unique ID of a list.

date_created.name: date_created
date_created.comment: The date and time that a list was created.

list_rating.name: list_rating
list_rating.comment: An auto-generated activity score for the list (0-5).

contact_address_1.name: contact_address_1
contact_address_1.comment: The street address for the list contact.

contact_address_2.name: contact_address_2
contact_address_2.comment: The street address for the list contact.

contact_city.name: contact_city
contact_city.comment: The city for the list contact.

contact_company.name: contact_company
contact_company.comment: The company name for the list.

contact_country.name: contact_country
contact_country.comment: A two-character ISO3166 country code. Defaults to US if invalid.

contact_state.name: contact_state
contact_state.comment: The state for the list contact.

contact_zip.name: contact_zip
contact_zip.comment: The postal or zip code for the list contact.

default_from_email.name: default_from_email
default_from_email.comment: The default from email for campaigns sent to a list.

default_from_name.name: default_from_name
default_from_name.comment: The default from name for campaigns sent to a list.

default_language.name: default_language
default_language.comment: The default language for a lists's forms.

default_subject.name: default_subject
default_subject.comment: The default subject line for campaigns sent to a list.

email_type_option.name: email_type_option
email_type_option.comment: Whether the list supports multiple formats for emails.  When set to true, subscribers can choose whether they want to receive HTML or plain-text emails.  When set to false, subscribers will receive HTML emails, with a plain-text alternative backup.

notify_on_subscribe.name: notify_on_subscribe
notify_on_subscribe.comment: The email address to send subscribe notifications to.

notify_on_unsubscribe.name: notify_on_unsubscribe
notify_on_unsubscribe.comment: The email address to send unsubscribe notifications to.

permission_reminder.name: permission_reminder
permission_reminder.comment: The permission reminder for a list.

subscribe_url_long.name: subscribe_url_long
subscribe_url_long.comment: The full version of a list's subscribe form (host will vary).

subscribe_url_short.name: subscribe_url_short
subscribe_url_short.comment: Mailchimp's EepURL shortened version of a list's subscribe form.

use_archive_bar.name: use_archive_bar
use_archive_bar.comment: Whether campaigns for this list use the Archive Bar in archives by default.

visibility.name: visibility
visibility.comment: Whether this list is public or private.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_mailchimp__members
metadata.comment: Each record represents a member in Mailchimp.

email_type.name: email_type
email_type.comment: Type of email this member asked to get ('html' or 'text').

status.name: status
status.comment: Subscriber's current status.  Possible Values: subscribed, unsubscribed, cleaned, pending, transactional, archived

list_id.name: list_id
list_id.comment: The ID of the list the member belongs to.

signup_timestamp.name: signup_timestamp
signup_timestamp.comment: The date and time the subscriber signed up for the list.

opt_in_timestamp.name: opt_in_timestamp
opt_in_timestamp.comment: The date and time the subscribe confirmed their opt-in status.

last_changed_timestamp.name: last_changed_timestamp
last_changed_timestamp.comment: The date and time the member's info was last changed.

country_code.name: country_code
country_code.comment: The member's unique code for the location country.

dstoff.name: dstoff
dstoff.comment: The member's offset for timezones where daylight saving time is observed.

gmtoff.name: gmtoff
gmtoff.comment: The member's time difference in hours from GMT.

opt_in_ip_address.name: opt_in_ip_address
opt_in_ip_address.comment: The IP address the subscriber used to confirm their opt-in status.

signup_ip_address.name: signup_ip_address
signup_ip_address.comment: IP address the subscriber signed up from.

member_rating.name: member_rating
member_rating.comment: Star rating for this member, between 1 and 5.

timezone.name: timezone
timezone.comment: The timezone for the member's location.

unique_email_id.name: unique_email_id
unique_email_id.comment: An identifier for the address across all of Mailchimp.
